title: Sedimentation Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5cj2l","text":"This protocol details sedimentation assay for alpha-synuclein fibrils.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: A sucrose cushion will minimize chances of the pellet being disturbed but may increase the chances of a-synuclein monomer ending up in the pellet fraction. Dilute 4 µL of 5 mg/mL α-synuclein PFFs to 40 µL in PBS in ultracentrifuge tubes.
Ultracentrifuge at 100000 x g, , ( 45000 rpm, , ) for 00:30:00 at 22 °C .
Remove 70 µL supernatant and add to new tube.
Add 60 µL 12.5% sucrose to the pellet and resuspend the pellet by pipetting.
Run samples on a 15% polyacrylamide gel. 20 µL or less of sample can be loaded. Loading less may make for a cleaner gel(no α-synuclein PFFs visible in the supernatant).
Dilute 4 µL of 5 mg/mL α-synuclein PFFs to 40 µL in PBS.
Ultracentrifuge at 100000 x g, , ( 45000 rpm, , ) for 00:30:00 at 25 °C .
Remove supernatant and dilute in 5x sample buffer.
Add 40 µL PBS to the pellet.
Resuspend pellet by pipetting up and down. Dilute in 5x sample buffer.
Run samples on a 15% polyacrylamide gel. 10 µL or less of sample can be loaded. Loading less may make for a cleaner gel (no α-synuclein PFFs visible in the supernatant).
creator: Michael Henderson
